Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Intermediate RedDelta Modified PlugX Infection Chain Operations targets healthcare organizations using spearphishing to gain initial access, followed by PowerShell execution, persistence via scheduled tasks, privilege escalation, defense evasion, and ultimately data exfiltration.

threat_actor_profile.campaign_or_group: name=RedDelta Modified PlugX Infection Chain Operations

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Users, Internet-facing assets, Internal networks
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email, Web, Phishing, Removable Media

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2024-12686 [CWE-22]: Vulnerability allowing remote code execution via spearphishing attachment
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 511 - Infiltration of Software Development Environment
stage.description: The threat actors crafted convincing spearphishing emails targeting employees within the healthcare organization. These emails contained malicious attachments exploiting CVE-2024-12686, enabling remote code execution upon opening.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-29059 [CWE-94]: Improper Control of Generically Named Objects leading to unauthorized access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 579 - Replace Winlogon Helper DLL
stage.description: Upon successful exploitation of the vulnerability in the previous stage, the attackers employed PowerShell to execute commands and establish persistence on compromised systems, leveraging CVE-2024-29059 to bypass security controls.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers created scheduled tasks to maintain persistent access to compromised systems, ensuring their presence remained undetected while they continued to explore the network and escalate privileges.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: To elevate privileges within the targeted systems, the threat actors exploited weaknesses in User Account Control (UAC) to gain higher-level access, allowing them to perform further actions unrestricted.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: To evade detection, the attackers signed malicious code with legitimate certificates obtained through unauthorized means, making it appear as if the code was from a trusted source.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-33106 [CWE-502]: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in PlugX RAT
stage.description: After successfully evading defenses, the attackers deployed the PlugX Remote Access Trojan (RAT) onto compromised systems, exploiting CVE-2023-33106 to facilitate command and control communication.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24 - Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: With unfettered access to sensitive data, the threat actors exfiltrated valuable information from the compromised healthcare organization, potentially resulting in significant financial loss and reputational damage.

Scenario.summary: RedDelta Modified PlugX Infection Chain Operations employs a sophisticated multi-stage attack against healthcare organizations, utilizing spearphishing, PowerShell, and advanced persistence techniques to ultimately exfiltrate sensitive data.
